steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content: A radian measure converts to degrees by multiplying by $\frac{180}{\pi}$. Convert $\frac{\pi}{12}$ first, then use the given equation $5n$ degrees.

steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$.
$$\frac{\pi}{12} \text{ rad} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{180}{12} = 15^{\circ}$$
So angle H measures 15 degrees.

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
We're told that angle H equals $5n$ degrees. Since we found that angle H = 15 degrees:
$$5n = 15$$
$$n = 3$$
A is correct.
